lesson.menu_title: A Little, a Few, Too Much -- The Budget Meeting
lesson.menu_desc: Run the numbers for the second international trip when the budget is limited: too much and too many for what is over the top, not enough for what is missing, and too + adjective for what is too expensive or too long -- with the words of money and a real budget negotiation with Bruno. Vocabulary: sufficient, limited, allocate, exceed, balance, priority, approval, spending. Expression: 'I agree / I disagree.' Idiom: 'Cut corners.' -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 13 -- A Little, a Few, Too Much
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 13 -- A Little, a Few, Too Much

listenings.text: Hi Maria Clara, it is Bruno. A quick update before our budget meeting. The news is not perfect. This year the budget is limited, and we do not have enough money for the whole delegation. There are too many requests, because every department wants a seat, but there are not enough approvals from the finance office. The spending on accommodation is too high, because the hotel near the center is too expensive. In my opinion, we should set a clear priority and allocate more money to the documents. We can't cut corners with the visas. Please think about it, and we talk tomorrow. Thank you.

Good morning. This is a short message from the finance office, with the rules for the delegation budget. Please listen before you plan the trip. First, the budget is limited this year, so every department has a maximum amount, and you should not exceed it. Second, the priority is documentation and travel. We allocate enough money for the flights and the visas, but only a little for extra materials. Third, the spending on dinners and gifts is too high in many reports, so please keep it low. And one more thing. If you need more than your amount, you must ask for approval one week before. Thank you, and please remember: a good plan keeps a healthy balance.

items.situation: The finance officer says: "You have a lot of requests this year." React, and say there is a problem with the approvals.
tips: There are too many requests, but not enough approvals.
too many = countable (requests). not enough = what is missing.

items.situation: The officer asks: "How is the spending on the hotel?" Say it is over the top, and give the reason.
tips: The spending on accommodation is too high.
The hotel near the center is too expensive.

items.situation: The officer says: "Can you cut the documentation time to save money?" Disagree, and use the idiom.
tips: I disagree. We can't cut corners with the visas.

items.situation: The officer asks: "Do you have enough money for the whole team?" Answer honestly with the budget.
tips: No, the budget is limited. We do not have enough money for the whole delegation.
not enough = there is a shortage.

items.situation: The officer says: "You are close to the maximum amount." Promise not to go over it.
tips: I agree. We will not exceed the budget.
exceed = to go over the limit.

items.situation: The officer asks: "What is your plan for the money?" Give the priority and where you allocate it.
tips: Our priority is documentation and travel, so we allocate more to the visas.
We keep a healthy balance and we don't cut corners.
